payload.text:
https://aistudio.google.com/apps/drive/15AilXHLCcaZ0F0prb3sF0kQvRwR047PJ?showPreview=true&showAssistant=true

Review this app for headers and footers and apply the exact same including the BuzzCraft logo this page

**Developing Header and Footer**

I'm currently focused on the details of integrating headers and footers into the "Constella" application, specifically matching the desired style. The goal is to make these elements seamlessly integrate with the existing design. I’m thinking about how to best implement these new features and to make sure that these additions feel like a natural part of the whole application.


**Designing App Structure**

I'm now deep in the design stage, focusing on the app's structure. My current priority is building `Header.tsx`, `Footer.tsx`, and `BeeIcon.tsx` components. The header is planned to be sticky with a black background and gold text, incorporating the BuzzCraft logo. For the footer, I'm aiming for a subtle, dark design with placeholder social links and copyright information.


**Implementing Full App Layout**

I'm now prioritizing the full app layout. My current focus is updating `App.tsx` to accommodate the `Header` and `Footer` components, ensuring they span the full width of the viewport. Also, I'm working on the `Header.tsx` and `Footer.tsx` components. I'm also planning to create a `BeeIcon` component for branding to align with the "Consistent Story AI Creator" title. I will keep using the "Constella" metadata name.


**Coding Core Components**

I'm now writing the necessary code. I'm prioritizing making the header fixed, for brand recognition during scrolling. I'll maintain a footer at the bottom. I'm focusing on high-contrast black and gold, with modern, clean typography and a geometric bee icon for the logo. I'm keeping the "Constella" metadata name.

entries.description: Add a stylized Bee icon for the BuzzCraft logo.

diffs.replacement:
import React from 'react';

export const BeeIcon: React.FC<React.SVGProps<SVGSVGElement>> = (props) => (
  <svg 
    viewBox="0 0 24 24" 
    fill="none" 
    xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" 
    className="h-8 w-8"
    {...props}
  >
    <path 
      d="M12 2L9 7H15L12 2Z" 
      fill="currentColor" 
    />
    <path 
      d="M7 10C7 8.34315 8.34315 7 10 7H14C15.6569 7 17 8.34315 17 10V14C17 15.6569 15.6569 17 14 17H10C8.34315 17 7 15.6569 7 14V10Z" 
      fill="currentColor" 
    />
    <path 
      d="M10 19L12 22L14 19H10Z" 
      fill="currentColor" 
    />
    <path 
      d="M17 9.5C18.5 8.5 21 8.5 22 10C23 11.5 21.5 14.5 18 14.5" 
      stroke="currentColor" 
      strokeWidth="1.5" 
      strokeLinecap="round" 
    />
    <path 
      d="M7 9.5C5.5 8.5 3 8.5 2 10C1 11.5 2.5 14.5 6 14.5" 
      stroke="currentColor" 
      strokeWidth="1.5" 
      strokeLinecap="round" 
    />
    <rect x="9" y="9" width="6" height="1.5" fill="black" />
    <rect x="9" y="12" width="6" height="1.5" fill="black" />
  </svg>
);

payload.text:
change the name from consistency engine to Buzzcraft "Constel AI"
add a button to generate a prompt, which lets the AI become creative based on whatever has already been inputted

**Refining Application Identity**

I'm now focused on rebranding BuzzCraft. "Consistency Engine" is out; "Constel AI" is in. This involves finding and replacing all instances of the old name within the application, ensuring a seamless transition to the new brand identity. Then, I will address adding the "Generate Prompt" button to enhance user experience.


**Adding Gemini Prompt Generation**

I'm making progress. I am adding a "Generate Prompt" button to `ControlPanel.tsx`, which will call a new Gemini API function. This API, implemented in `geminiService.ts`, uses `gemini-3-flash-preview` to creatively generate story prompts using existing details. I'm also addressing updates in `App.tsx` for state management.


**Enhancing AI Prompt Generation**

I've just added a `suggestPrompt` function in `geminiService.ts` using `gemini-3-flash-preview`, now that I have determined its use-case as a "Basic Text Task." The `ControlPanel.tsx` now has a "Magic Prompt" button next to the "Add Prompt" button. This button has loading states for generating. I am now working on handling `handleGenerateCreativePrompt` and passing it to `ControlPanel` in `App.tsx`. Next step will be the visual enhancements for that button. Finally, I will address the XML updates. I am using the updated prompt: "Given a story titled '{storyName}'... "

payload.text:
- remove the footer and the links in the header and leave only the logo and app name
- remove the word BuzzCraft and rename the app CONSTELLA, make the logo a constellation of stars

**Considering a Rebrand**

I'm currently focused on the rebranding strategy for the application. The plan is to rename "BUZZCRAFT" and "BuzzCraft" to "CONSTELLA". I'll ensure consistency by updating the subtitle to "Constel AI" where necessary, since that's already the intended direction. Next steps include a logo overhaul.


**Implementing Visual Changes**

I've been working on the visual overhaul. The BeeIcon is being replaced with a new StarConstellationIcon, and I'm simplifying the Header: the logo and name "CONSTELLA" are all that remain. The Footer has been completely removed from the layout. Style updates are underway to match the new name and star theme, ensuring CONSTELLA is prominent.


**Developing the Logo**

I'm now refining the constellation logo. I'm focusing on a design with connected dots and stars, and I'll be implementing changes across multiple files. The `StarConstellationIcon.tsx` is new, and the `Header.tsx` is being simplified and rebranded. I'll remove the footer from `App.tsx`, and I'm updating `index.html` to reflect the new name, including the title. I'll also check `metadata.json`. The `Header.tsx` update replaces "BUZZCRAFT" with "CONSTELLA" for brand consistency.

entries.description: Create a new icon representing a constellation of stars for the CONSTELLA brand.

diffs.replacement:
import React from 'react';

export const StarConstellationIcon: React.FC<React.SVGProps<SVGSVGElement>> = (props) => (
  <svg 
    viewBox="0 0 24 24" 
    fill="none" 
    xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" 
    className="h-8 w-8"
    {...props}
  >
    {/* Connection lines */}
    <path d="M4 18L9 14" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="1" strokeDasharray="2 2" opacity="0.4" />
    <path d="M9 14L12 6" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="1" strokeDasharray="2 2" opacity="0.4" />
    <path d="M12 6L18 10" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="1" strokeDasharray="2 2" opacity="0.4" />
    <path d="M18 10L20 4" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="1" strokeDasharray="2 2" opacity="0.4" />
    <path d="M9 14L15 20" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="1" strokeDasharray="2 2" opacity="0.4" />

    {/* Stars */}
    <circle cx="4" cy="18" r="1.5" fill="currentColor">
        <animate attributeName="opacity" values="0.4;1;0.4" dur="2s" repeatCount="indefinite" />
    </circle>
    <circle cx="9" cy="14" r="2" fill="currentColor">
        <animate attributeName="opacity" values="1;0.4;1" dur="2.5s" repeatCount="indefinite" />
    </circle>
    <circle cx="12" cy="6" r="2.5" fill="currentColor">
        <animate attributeName="opacity" values="0.6;1;0.6" dur="3s" repeatCount="indefinite" />
    </circle>
    <circle cx="18" cy="10" r="1.8" fill="currentColor">
        <animate attributeName="opacity" values="1;0.5;1" dur="2.2s" repeatCount="indefinite" />
    </circle>
    <circle cx="20" cy="4" r="1.2" fill="currentColor">
        <animate attributeName="opacity" values="0.3;1;0.3" dur="1.8s" repeatCount="indefinite" />
    </circle>
    <circle cx="15" cy="20" r="1.5" fill="currentColor">
        <animate attributeName="opacity" values="0.7;1;0.7" dur="2.7s" repeatCount="indefinite" />
    </circle>
  </svg>
);

entries.description: Rename branding to CONSTELLA, replace logo with StarConstellationIcon, and remove all navigation links.
Remove the Footer component and its import to simplify the UI.
Update the document title to CONSTELLA AI.

payload.text:
- when generating a single character, the loading animation should occur only in that small character window
- the 4 character names should be One, Two, Three and Four
- allow the user to add up to 10 characters
- change the overall layout to the Generated Images window appear at the top and the Generations Setting window appear underneath (horizontally) with individual tabs to switch between: Story & Series (call this tab "General"), Character References (call this tab "Cast Members"), Style & Format (put this tab second or rather immediately right of the General tab and call it "Output Settings"), and Prompts (call this tab "Scenes List"). 
- In the General tab:
  1. add a 3rd text field fo "Scene Number" and ensure proper file saving protocols: "Scene[number]_[StoryName]_[SeriesName]
  2. add a larger description text box with the title "Synopsis", where the user can enter up to 300 words of overall storyline
   2.1. Under the Synopsis box add two small buttons.
     2.1.1 One to "Generate", which will generate a riveting short storyline with the characters already entered or with fictitious, yet unnamed characters
     2.1.2 Another to "Enhance", which will take the user input and rewrite it to be a better storyline, with more opportunity for story diversion to keep the overall Series engaging throughout.
- In the Cast Members tab, remove the option to rotate the image and have only Upload, Download, Generate and Prompt (this lets the user prompt the image they want. By default image 1 or "One" will always be the reference image in terms of artistic style)
 - In the Output Settings tab:
   1. For the Image Style dropdown, add an option to "use reference image one", which will strictly apply only the same style as the image currently uploaded to box One. Whenever an image is generated anywhere in the app, the setting currently style applied here will apply to the newly generated image(s). Therefore, allow the user to set a lock on this option, as well as the aspect ratio
- In the Scenes List tab:
  1. Presume at least 6 scenes by default, since the window will be horizontal, add them in two columns of 3 each until the user adds more
- The Add Step button should be called "Add Scene" - the user can add up to 20 scenes maximum.
- The "Render Sequence" button should be a nice contrasting color like #55DDE0 but you select the most appropriate to match the vibe
- the background should be black

**Refining Application Parameters**

I'm now focusing on specific changes for the "CONSTELLA AI" app. The loading state for character generation needs to be localized to each character slot. I'm also setting the initial characters to "One", "Two", "Three", and "Four", with a maximum of ten. Finally, I've got a layout change in the works.


**Structuring the New Layout**

I've just structured the layout, organizing it with generated images at the top and settings horizontally at the bottom, using tabs for clarity. The "General" tab focuses on the story's overall details, with options for "Series Name," "Story Name," a synopsis, and generation buttons. The "Output Settings" tab immediately to the right, allows "Image Style" selections with an "Aspect Ratio," as well as "Lock" buttons. Characters are now in the "Cast Members" tab, and scenes in the last.


**Implementing Tabbed Controls**

I'm now fully committed to the horizontal tabbed layout at the bottom of the new design, and that's the active focus. I'm updating `CharacterSlot.tsx` with a prompt input and individual loading states. GeminiService will generate synopses and enhance them, and it will handle the reference image. The results will now be up top. I'm tracking loading per character ID and will pass a slot-specific `isSlotLoading` prop to `CharacterSlot`.


**Revising Layout Strategy**

I'm now reconsidering the layout, moving from horizontal tabs back to a vertical stack. I'll need to redesign the character list to fit this change. I'll use a `ControlPanel` element at the bottom, and a `ResultsDisplay` at the top, as before. I have also decided to limit to ten characters and twenty scenes. I'm adding a prompt field to the `Character` type, and some GeminiService functions. I'll call those functions with the character names from `generateSynopsis`.

entries.description: Update Character interface to include a prompt and add USE_REFERENCE_ONE to ImageStyle.
Add synopsis generation and enhancement functions. Update image generation to handle style reference from Character One.
Redesign App layout to horizontal stack with Results on top and Settings at bottom. Increase character limit to 10, scene limit to 20. Implement character-specific loading and new filename logic.
Complete redesign of ControlPanel to a horizontal tabbed layout at the bottom. Implement General, Output Settings, Cast Members, and Scenes List tabs with requested features.
Remove rotation button. Add prompt input field. Implement local loading indicator. Restrict UI to Upload, Download, Generate, and Prompt functions.
Update file saving protocols to match requested Scene[number]_[StoryName]_[SeriesName] format.

payload.text: remove the subheading CONSTEL AI and replace it with a clever tagline cta that gets the user excited to start creating. Try to focus on the end-goal or finished product as an incentive.

**Refining Branding Elements**

I've just focused on streamlining the CONSTELLA AI branding. The "Constel AI" subheading is out, and I'm now tasked with crafting a compelling tagline or call-to-action to excite the user. The goal is to highlight the end product.


**Defining the New Tagline**

I've been honing in on a tagline for STELLA. I explored several options, finally settling on "Your Story, Rendered in Perfect Continuity." The goal is to convey the consistent character creation. I will need to update `components/Header.tsx` to reflect the new tagline.


**Finalizing Tagline Updates**

I've iterated on the tagline again, aiming for maximum user engagement. "Bring Your Legends to Life with Flawless Continuity" is the current frontrunner, though I'm still weighing a variant: "From Spark to Saga: Your Visual Masterpiece Starts Here." I've confirmed that `components/Header.tsx` requires modification, and I've reviewed the code block to be updated.

entries.description: Remove the "Constel AI" subheading and replace it with an exciting, goal-oriented tagline that encourages the user to create their story.

payload.text: I like it but make it even shorter, say "CRAFT IN FLAWLESS CONTINUITY"

**Refining Tagline**

I've zeroed in on the tagline update for `Header.tsx`. The aim is to replace the existing one with "CRAFT IN FLAWLESS CONTINUITY". I am working to remove the `Footer.tsx` and any associated dependencies.


**Confirming Code Analysis**

I've verified that `Footer.tsx` is indeed unused in `App.tsx` and can be disregarded. My focus remains on updating the tagline within `Header.tsx`. The core task is to implement the new tagline, "CRAFT IN FLAWLESS CONTINUITY." This has been confirmed. I am ready to integrate the changes into `Header.tsx`.


**Implementing Tagline Update**

I'm now integrating the new tagline directly into `Header.tsx`. I've ensured "CRAFT IN FLAWLESS CONTINUITY" is correctly formatted and implemented. I am focusing on the text replacement, and am keeping the other formatting of the file the same. The change has been committed.

entries.description: Update the header tagline to a shorter, more impactful CTA: "CRAFT IN FLAWLESS CONTINUITY".

payload.text:
- The Render Sequence button should just be called "RENDER"
- On Mobile, not all tabs, buttons and windows are fitting correctly. The user should NEVER have to scroll left or right, only up and down.
- Make each tab heading and tab size small enough to allow for all 4 tabs to fit from left to right
- the cast members should be in 2 columns, scrolling vertically (on mobile)
- on mobile, put the Render button just above the tabs (just below the output window
SCENES LIST:
- Add a smaller sub-window with the option to select active characters, that will be used for the next magic prompt generation
- The magic prompt button should be a small icon (only) next to each prompt, next to and similar to the delete icon
- The magic Prompt button under all the scenes, should populate each empty scene field with a unique new scene for all the currently selected "active characters"

**Updating UI/UX Plans**

Okay, I'm focusing on the Constella AI app UI/UX updates. I've got a couple of key items: "Render Sequence" has been changed to "RENDER." I'm also addressing mobile optimization, specifically eliminating horizontal scrolling and ensuring tabs fit horizontally. I am also currently working on cast members.


**Refining Magic Prompt Logic**

Okay, I'm now working to refine the Magic Prompt logic, integrating active character selection for each scene. I'm focusing on adding a sub-window to select active characters. Also, I'm working to implement individual Magic Prompt buttons per scene (icon only), plus the main "Magic Prompt" button should fill any empty scenes with unique new ones based on the active characters.


**Revising Character Selection**

I'm now revising the Active Characters selection process. I'm focusing on adding a sub-component within the Scenes List tab, as requested. I'm also exploring leveraging the existing `isSelected` property in `CastMembers` or if a separate selection is needed. I'm also updating the bulk Magic Prompt button behavior and working on implementing per-scene Magic Prompt icons. Further, I'm working to refine the mobile layout, especially for the "RENDER" button positioning and 2-column view for `CharacterSlot.tsx`


**Optimizing Mobile Layout**

I'm now focusing on optimizing the mobile layout of the `ControlPanel`. I am going to move the "Render" button to the top of the panel, just below the results display, in keeping with user feedback. Additionally, I'll revise the `CastMembers` display for mobile, using a 2-column, vertically scrolling grid instead of the current horizontal scroll. Finally, I will also implement changes for scenes list magic prompt, including individual icons, a bulk fill button and active character selection.


**Defining Active Characters**

I'm now defining the `activeCharacterIds` state specifically for the Scenes List to support the bulk and single suggestions. I'm also addressing the magic prompt button, which should be a small icon next to each prompt using the `SparklesIcon`. I am planning to reduce tab heading/size to ensure all 4 tabs fit horizontally, with padding and font-size adjusted for small screens. I'm also planning to explore the use of an `isSelected` property.


**Implementing Magic Prompt Functions**

Now, I'm diving into the code. I've pinpointed the key files: `App.tsx`, `ControlPanel.tsx`, `services/geminiService.ts`, and potentially `types.ts` and `ResultsDisplay.tsx`. My current focus is refining `suggestCreativePrompt` to handle active characters and generate multiple unique scenes. Specifically, I plan to revise the `geminiService.ts` function for efficiency, considering whether to call Gemini once for a list of scenes, or N times, depending on needs.

entries.description: Ensure ImageStyle and AspectRatio are up to date.
Add suggestBulkScenes to generate multiple unique scenes for empty fields.
Updated handlers for per-scene magic prompt and bulk magic prompt. Added state for promptActiveCharacterIds.
Redesigned for mobile responsiveness, fixed Render button location on mobile, and updated Scenes List with active character selection and individual magic prompt icons.

payload.text:
- rename output settings to "Output"
- rename Scenes List to "Scenes"
- rename Cast Members to "Cast"
- Under General, remove the Scene Number field

**Refining Tab Titles**

I've been focusing on streamlining the application's interface. My recent attention centers on tab naming. The goal is clarity and efficiency. I'm planning to rename "Output Settings" to "Output", "Scenes List" to "Scenes", and "Cast Members" to "Cast" to enhance user flow, reducing unnecessary verbiage and improve ease of use. I will also be removing the Scene Number Field from the General tab.


**Addressing Scene Number Removal**

I'm now deep in the code, tackling the "Scene Number" field removal. My initial approach is to ensure file naming in `ResultsDisplay` doesn't break, likely defaulting the index if the `sceneNumber` state is removed. I've also verified that the Render button stays where it should. I'm also now ensuring the Scenes list behaves as specified in the prior turns. Finally, the "General" tab has been reduced to just series/story name and synopsis.


**Updating UI Elements**

My current focus is on the user interface changes. I've decided to remove the "Scene Number" input from the `ControlPanel`'s General tab, as instructed. I'm choosing to keep the `sceneNumber` state in `App.tsx` for now, defaulting it to 1, as the filename structure still uses it. I will keep the existing tab naming convention. The layout changes remain as specified.


**Implementing Filename Logic**

I'm now zeroing in on the file naming structure after removing the scene number input. I've decided to derive the scene number directly from the iteration index in the rendering process, starting at 1. The filename logic in `ResultsDisplay` is updated to handle this change, ensuring compatibility with the existing "[Scene[number]_[StoryName]_[SeriesName]" format. I'm also confirming that tab IDs will match the new tab names for consistency. I'm taking another look at the cast tab's mobile display as well.

entries.description: Remove sceneNumber state as the field is being removed from the UI. Scene numbers will now be handled automatically based on their position in the list.
Rename tabs to "General", "Output", "Cast", and "Scenes". Remove the Scene Number field from the General tab. Improve tab size and fitting for mobile devices.
Remove sceneNumber prop and update filename logic to use the individual image's scene index (1-based).
